INTERNATIONAL ARBITRATION LAW* Law No. 4686 of 21 June 2001. Official Gazette of 5 July 2001 No. 24453. In effect 5 July 2001. Unofficial translation by Prof. Ali Yesilirmak, Ms Utku Cosar and Dr Dogan Gultutan. The notes and explanations in square brackets have been added to the text by the translators solely for the purpose of providing the reader with further information and/or assistance.

sections.title: General Provisions
sections.text: Chapter One. General Provisions Article 1 Purpose and scope The purpose of this Law is to regulate procedural and substantive matters concerning international arbitration. This Law shall apply in cases where the dispute involves a foreign element and Turkey is determined as the place of arbitration, or where the provisions of this Law are agreed upon by the parties or the arbitral tribunal. Articles 5 and 6 of this Law shall apply even in cases where the place of arbitration determined is located outside Turkey. This Law shall not apply to disputes related to real rights (ayni haklar) concerning immovable property located in Turkey and disputes that are not subject to the parties' will. This Law shall also apply to the resolution, through international arbitration, of disputes arising from concession contracts relating to public services and which involve a foreign element, pursuant to the Law Concerning the Principles to be adhered to in Respect of Arbitration Proceedings Arising from Concession Contracts Concerning Public Services, Law No. 4501 of 21 January 2000. The provisions of international conventions to which Turkey is a party are reserved. Article 2 Foreign element The existence of any of the following circumstances shall indicate that the dispute involves a foreign element, in which case the arbitration shall be considered as international. 1. Where the parties to the arbitration agreement have their domiciles or habitual residences or places of business in different States. 2. Where the parties have their domicile, habitual residence or place of business in a State other than: a. the place of arbitration, specified in, or determined pursuant to, the arbitration agreement; [or] b. the place where a substantial part of the obligations arising from the underlying contract is to be performed or the place most closely connected to the subject matter of the dispute. 3. Where at least one of the shareholders of the company that is a party to the underlying contract constituting the basis for the arbitration agreement has brought foreign capital [into Turkey] in accordance with the laws concerning the encouragement of foreign capital or where the execution of a loan and/or guarantee agreement(s) for the purposes of procuring capital from abroad is required for the implementation of the said underlying contract. 4. Where the underlying contract constituting the basis for the arbitration agreement or the legal relationship results in the cross-border movement of capital or goods. The provisions of Law No. 4501 of 21 January 2000 are reserved. Article 3 Competent Court and Extent of Court Intervention In respect of court intervention or assistance foreseen in this Law, the competent court shall be the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) located at the respondent's domicile, habitual residence or place of business; where the respondent does not have its domicile, habitual residence or place of business in Turkey, the competent court shall be the Istanbul Civil Court of First Instance (Istanbul Asliye Hukuk Mahkemesi). In respect of matters concerning international arbitration, the courts may only intervene in accordance with the provisions of this Law.

sections.title: Arbitration Agreement
sections.text: Chapter Two. Arbitration Agreement Article 4 Definition and Form An arbitration agreement is an agreement by the parties to submit to arbitration all or certain disputes, whether contractual or otherwise, which have arisen or which may arise between them in respect of a current legal relationship. An arbitration agreement may be in the form of an arbitration clause in the underlying contract or in the form of a separate agreement. The arbitration agreement shall be in writing. In order for the written form requirement to be considered satisfied, the arbitration agreement must be recorded in a written document signed by the parties or in a means of communication such as letters, telegram, telex, facsimile exchanged by the parties or in electronic environment, or the existence of a written arbitration agreement is alleged in the statement of claim and this is not objected by the respondent in its statement of defence. A reference [in the underlying contract] to a document containing an arbitration clause shall constitute an arbitration agreement provided that such reference was for the purpose of incorporating the clause into the underlying contract. The arbitration agreement shall be valid if it is in accordance with the law agreed by the parties as applicable to the arbitration agreement, failing such agreement, with Turkish law. No objection shall be made against the arbitration agreement on the basis of the invalidity of the underlying contract or that the arbitration agreement relates to a dispute not yet arisen. Article 5 Arbitration objection and agreement to arbitrate before the court If an action is brought before the court concerning a matter which is the subject of an arbitration agreement, the counter-party may raise an arbitration objection. The provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure relating to preliminary objections shall apply to the assertion of arbitration objections and the resolution of disputes concerning the validity of the arbitration agreement. In case the court upholds an arbitration objection, it shall dismiss the action on procedural grounds. In case the parties agree to arbitrate during court proceedings, the case file shall be sent by the court to the relevant arbitral tribunal. Article 6 Interim measures of protection and interim attachments It is not incompatible with an arbitration agreement for a party to request, before or during arbitral proceedings, from a court an interim measure of protection or an interim attachment, and for a court to grant such measure or attachment. Unless otherwise agreed, the arbitral tribunal may, at the request of a party, order interim measures of protection or interim attachments during arbitral proceedings. The arbitral tribunal may subject granting interim measures of protection or interim attachment to the provision of an appropriate security. The arbitral tribunal shall not be empowered to grant interim measures of protection or attachments [a] that are required to be enforced through execution offices or to be executed via other official authorities or [b] that bind third parties. Where a party fails to comply with the interim measure of protection or attachment order made by the arbitral tribunal, the counter-party may request the competent court's assistance in connection with the issuance of an order for interim measure of protection or an interim attachment. The competent court may, where required, hear [the case in question] through a substitute court. The parties' right to make requests in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure and the Code of Execution is reserved. Any interim measures of protection or interim attachments granted by the court prior to or during arbitral proceedings on the application of a party shall automatically cease to have effect once the award of the arbitral tribunal becomes enforceable or the arbitral tribunal dismisses the claim.

sections.title: Arbitral Tribunal's Appointment, Challenge, Responsibility, Termination of its Duties and its Competence
sections.text: Chapter Three. Arbitral Tribunal's Appointment, Challenge, Responsibility, Termination of its Duties and its Competence Article 7 Number of arbitrators, their appointment, challenge, responsibility, termination of duties and competence A) The parties are entitled to determine the number of arbitrators. However, the number shall be an odd number. Where the parties have not determined the number of arbitrators, three arbitrators shall be appointed. B) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the following principles apply to the appointment of arbitrators: 1. Only natural persons can be appointed as arbitrators. 2. Where a sole arbitrator is to be appointed and the parties are unable to agree on the arbitrator, the arbitrator shall be appointed, upon request of a party, by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi). 3. Where three arbitrators are to be appointed, each party shall appoint one arbitrator and the two co-arbitrators thus appointed shall appoint the third arbitrator. Where a party fails to appoint an arbitrator within thirty days of receipt of a request to do so from the other party, or where the two co-arbitrators fail to agree on the third arbitrator within thirty days of their appointment, the appointment shall be made, upon request of a party, by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi). The third arbitrator appointed shall be the chairman of the arbitral tribunal. 4. Where more than three arbitrators are to be appointed, the arbitrators who are to appoint the final arbitrator shall be appointed by the parties in equal numbers in accordance with the procedure set forth in the above paragraph. Where, despite an agreement on the appointment procedure relating to arbitrators; 1. a party fails to comply with that agreement; 2. the parties or the arbitrators appointed by them are unable to agree under such procedure in respect of arbitrator appointment, despite being required to decide jointly on the arbitrator appointment under the agreed procedure; [or] 3. a third party, institution or body empowered to appoint arbitrators fails to appoint the arbitrator(s), the appointment of the arbitral tribunal shall be made, upon a party request, by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi). The civil court of first instance's decision rendered pursuant to these sub-articles, upon hearing the parties where necessary, shall be final. The court of first instance, in appointing an arbitrator, shall give due regard to: [1] the parties' agreement, [2] the principles regarding [the requirement for] arbitrator's independence and impartiality, and the need for the sole arbitrator or, where there are to be three arbitrators, for two arbitrators not to be of the same nationality as a party in cases where the parties are of different nationalities. The above procedure shall similarly apply where more than three arbitrators are to be appointed. C) Any person offered appointment as an arbitrator shall, prior to accepting appointment, disclose any circumstances likely to give rise to justifiable doubts as to its impartiality or independence. Provided that the parties have not already been informed, the arbitrator shall inform the parties of any such circumstances thereafter arising without delay. An arbitrator may be challenged in cases where: [1] it does not possess the qualifications required pursuant to the parties' agreement, [2] a ground for challenge foreseen in the arbitration procedure agreed upon by the parties exists, or [3] circumstances which give rise to justifiable doubts as to its impartiality or independence have arisen. D) The parties are entitled to agree on the procedure in respect of challenges against arbitrators. A party who intends to challenge an arbitrator may, within thirty days following the constitution of the arbitral tribunal or its awareness of the circumstance that may entitle a challenge against the arbitrator, submit its challenge, which challenge must be notified to the counter party by writing. A party who challenges one or more arbitrators shall notify the arbitral tribunal of its request and reasons for the challenge. Where the challenge is dismissed, the requesting party may apply to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi), within thirty days of becoming aware of the dismissal decision, to request an order that the arbitral tribunal's dismissal decision be revoked and the arbitrator(s) be removed. Any request to remove the entire arbitral tribunal or the decision-making majority may only be made to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi). The civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi)'s decisions rendered pursuant to this sub-article are final. Where the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) orders the removal of the entire arbitral tribunal or the decision-making majority, the arbitration proceedings shall terminate. Unless the arbitrator(s) has been designated by name in the arbitration agreement, the arbitrator appointment process shall be repeated. E) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, an arbitrator who accepts appointment shall be obliged to indemnify the parties for any damages suffered due to its avoidance of its duties without a justified reason. F) If an arbitrator becomes de jure or de facto unable to perform its functions, timely or at all, its mandate shall terminate upon its withdrawal or the parties agreement to that effect. Either party may request the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) to decide on the termination of the arbitrator's mandate should the parties disagree as to whether circumstances exist to require the arbitrator to withdraw from office. The decision rendered by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) shall be final. The arbitrator's withdrawal from office or the counter party's consent to the termination of the arbitrator's mandate shall not constitute the acceptance as to the existence of grounds for challenge. G) Where the mandate of an arbitrator terminates for any reason whatsoever, a replacement arbitrator shall be appointed in accordance with the rules that were applicable to the appointment of the arbitrator being replaced. The replacement of one or more arbitrators shall not suspend the arbitration period. However, where the arbitration agreement designates the arbitrator(s) by name, the arbitration proceedings shall terminate where the mandate of the arbitral tribunal or the decision-making majority terminates for any reason whatsoever. H) The arbitral tribunal may rule on its own jurisdiction, including any objections with respect to the existence or validity of the arbitration agreement. When determining such, the arbitration clause, where forming part of an underlying contract, shall be treated independent of the other terms of the underlying contract. A decision by the arbitral tribunal declaring the underlying contract null and void shall not entail ipso jure the invalidity of the arbitration agreement [contained within it]. Any challenge against the arbitral tribunal's jurisdiction shall be raised with the first statement of defence at the latest. The parties are not precluded from raising such a challenge due to their appointment of the arbitrator(s) or participation in the appointment process. Any challenge against the arbitral tribunal on the basis that it has exceeded the scope of its authority shall not be valid unless raised immediately. The arbitral tribunal may, in respect of either case described above, admit a delayed challenge where it considers the delay justified. The arbitral tribunal shall determine an objection relating to its lack of jurisdiction as a preliminary issue. Where the arbitral tribunal determines that it has jurisdiction, it shall proceed with the arbitration and render its award.

sections.title: Arbitral Procedure
sections.text: Chapter Four. Arbitral Procedure Article 8 Determination of the rules of procedure, equal treatment of parties and their representation A) Subject to the mandatory provisions of this Law, the parties are entitled to determine the rules of arbitral procedure to be applied by the arbitral tribunal, or determine such by way of reference to a law or international or institutional arbitration rules. Absent such party agreement, the arbitral tribunal shall conduct the arbitral proceedings in accordance with the provisions of this Law. B) The parties possess equal rights and authority in respect of the arbitral proceedings. The parties shall be afforded the [full] opportunity to assert their claims and defences. The parties may also be represented in arbitration proceedings by foreign natural or legal persons. This provision shall not apply in respect of requests made to a court concerning arbitration. Article 9 Place of arbitration The place of arbitration shall be freely determined by the parties or the arbitral institution agreed upon. Absent such agreement, the place of arbitration shall be determined by the arbitral tribunal having regard to the circumstances of the case. The arbitral tribunal may meet at a place other than the place of arbitration where the circumstances of the arbitral proceedings so require, provided that prior notice is given to the parties. Article 10 Commencement, period and language of arbitration, statements of claim and defence, and the terms of reference A) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, an arbitration shall be deemed commenced [1] on the date a request is made for the appointment of the arbitrator(s) to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) or to the person, institution or body the parties have agreed upon to appoint the arbitrator(s); [2] where both parties are responsible for the appointment of the arbitrators pursuant to the [arbitration] agreement, on the date the claimant notifies the counter-party of its arbitrator appointment and invites the counter-party to appoint its own arbitrator; or [3] where the arbitration agreement designates the arbitrators by name, on the date the counter-party receives the request for arbitration. Where a party has obtained an interim measure of protection or an interim attachment from a court, it shall commence arbitration proceedings within thirty days [as from the date the measure or attachment is granted]. Otherwise, the interim measure or the interim attachment shall automatically cease to have any effect. B) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the arbitral tribunal shall render its award on the merits within one year starting from the date of appointment of the arbitrator in cases with sole arbitrator, within one year starting from the date of the issuance of the first meeting minutes by the arbitral tribunal in cases with more than one arbitrator. The period of arbitration may be extended by party agreement, failing which by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) upon application by either party. Where the request for extension is dismissed, the arbitral proceedings shall terminate on the date the period of arbitration expires. The court's decision [on extension] shall be final. C) The language of the arbitration shall be Turkish or any other language that is the official language of a state recognized by the Republic of Turkey. Absent party agreement as regards the language or languages of the [arbitral] proceedings, such shall be determined by the arbitral tribunal. Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or determined by the arbitral tribunal in its relevant interim decision on the matter, such language(s) shall be utilised in respect of the parties' all written statements, the hearing(s), the arbitral tribunal's interim or final awards, and also in its [all other] written communications. The arbitral tribunal may order that any documents relied upon by the parties shall be accompanied by a translation into the language(s) used in the arbitration proceedings. D) Within the time period agreed by the parties or as determined by the arbitral tribunal, [1] the claimant shall submit to the arbitral tribunal its statement of claim containing [the particulars as to] the parties' names, titles and addresses, its legal representative, the arbitration clause or agreement, the contract or legal relationship giving rise or relating to the dispute, the circumstances upon which the claim is based, the subject matter of the dispute, the amount in dispute and its prayer for relief; [2] the respondent shall similarly submit to the arbitral tribunal its statement of defence. The parties may submit together with their statements written evidence on which they rely and may refer to the evidence they intend to submit at a later stage. Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, either party may amend or expand its claim or defence during the course of the arbitral proceedings, unless the arbitral tribunal considers it inappropriate to allow such amending or expansion having regard to its belated nature or on the ground that such will cause an unjustifiable and significant difficulty to the counter-party, and having regard to the other relevant circumstances. The claim or defence, cannot be amended or expanded where such would exceed the scope of the arbitration agreement. E) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the arbitral tribunal shall, following the filing of the statements of claim and defence, draw up its terms of reference. The terms of reference shall contain such particulars as the parties' names and titles, their addresses for notification during arbitral proceedings, a summary of their claims and defences, their prayers for relief, explanations as regards the dispute, the names, titles, and addresses of the arbitrators, the place of arbitration, the period of arbitration and the date of its commencement, explanations as regards the procedural provisions applicable to the dispute, and whether the arbitrators have authority to act as amiable compositeur. The terms of reference shall be signed by the arbitral tribunal and the parties. Article 11 Hearing and on-paper arbitral proceedings, loss of party status, non-participation of a party in arbitral proceedings A) The arbitral tribunal shall decide whether to hold hearings for the reasons such as the presentation of evidence, the submission of oral arguments or requests for explanations from experts, or whether to conduct the arbitral proceedings solely on the basis of review of documents. Unless the parties have agreed that no hearings shall be held, the arbitral tribunal shall hold a hearing(s) at an appropriate stage of the proceedings where requested by a party. The arbitral tribunal shall provide notice to the parties sufficiently in advance concerning the date of any site-inspection relating to the dispute, expert examination or any hearing and meeting of the arbitral tribunal to be held for the purposes of examining other evidence, and as to the consequences of the failure to attend such. All statements, information or other documents submitted to the arbitral tribunal by one party shall be communicated to the parties. B) Where a party to the arbitration loses its party status, the arbitral tribunal shall postpone the arbitral proceedings and notify the concerned for the purposes of the continuation of the proceedings. In this case, the arbitration period shall stop to run during the postponement. The arbitral proceedings shall terminate where, within six months [following the postponement], [1] no notification is made [within the meaning of the above sub-article] or [2] despite such notification being made, the notifying party fails to notify in express terms the counter-party or the arbitral tribunal of its intention to continue with the arbitral proceedings. C) Where a party does not participate in the arbitration proceedings, the following provisions shall apply: 1. Where the claimant fails to submit its statement of claim within the period provided without a valid reason, the arbitral tribunal shall terminate the arbitral proceedings. 2. Where the statement of claim is not in accordance with the first paragraph of Article 10(D) and the deficiency is not remedied within the period determined by the arbitral tribunal, the tribunal shall terminate the arbitral proceedings. 3. Where the respondent fails to submit its statement of defence, the arbitral proceedings shall continue, and the failure shall not be considered as constituting the respondent's acceptance of the claimant's claims. 4. Where either party fails to attend a hearing or produce its evidence without a valid reason, the arbitral tribunal may continue the arbitral proceedings and render its award on the basis of the evidence submitted. Article 12 Expert appointment by the arbitral tribunal, taking of evidence, laws applicable to the merits of the dispute and settlement A) The arbitral tribunal may: 1. appoint one or more experts to produce a report on specific issues determined by the arbitral tribunal; 2. require the parties to make necessary explanations to the expert(s), [produce or] provide access to the expert(s) to any relevant information or documents; 3. order an on-site inspection to be conducted. Unless otherwise agreed, where a party so requests or the arbitral tribunal considers it necessary, the expert(s) shall, after delivery of its written or oral reports [on the relevant matters], attend the hearing to which they are invited. At the hearing, the parties shall have the opportunity to put questions to the expert(s) and to have their party-appointed experts heard on matters in dispute. B) The parties shall submit their evidence within the time period determined by the arbitral tribunal. The arbitral tribunal may request assistance from the court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) in respect of the taking evidence. In such cases the court shall apply the provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure. C) The arbitral tribunal shall render its award in accordance with the terms of the contract between the parties and the law chosen by them to govern the merits of the dispute. In the interpretation of contractual terms and when filling any lacuna in respect of such, the commercial usage and customs and business practices relating to the chosen law shall be considered. Unless otherwise stated, a choice as to the laws of a state shall be construed as a choice as regards the substantive laws of that state, and not its conflict of laws rules or its rules of procedure. In the absence of party agreement as regards the law applicable to the merits of the dispute, the arbitral tribunal shall render its award according to the substantive laws of the state it considers most closely connected to the dispute. The arbitral tribunal shall decide ex aequo et bono or as amiable compositeur only where expressly so authorised by the parties. D) Where the parties settle the dispute during arbitral proceedings, the arbitral proceedings shall be terminated. The arbitral tribunal shall, upon request and where such request is deemed appropriate, record the settlement in the form of an arbitral award [on terms agreed]. Article 13 Arbitral tribunal's decision-making procedure, and termination of arbitral proceedings A) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the arbitral tribunal shall decide by a majority of all its members. Where authorised by the parties or the other members of the arbitral tribunal, the presiding arbitrator may determine certain issues concerning the arbitral procedure on its own. B) The arbitral proceedings shall terminate upon the issuance of a final award or on the occurrence of any of the following circumstances: 1. The claimant's withdrawal of its claim, unless the respondent objects and the arbitral tribunal accepts that the latter possesses a legal interest in the final resolution of the dispute; 2. The parties agree to terminate the arbitral proceedings; 3. The arbitral tribunal considers the continuation of the arbitral proceedings unnecessary or impossible due to any other reasons; 4. The court dismisses a request to extend the arbitration period, pursuant to the second paragraph of Article 10(B); 5. The arbitral tribunal fails to render its award with unanimity, despite the parties' agreement to that effect; 6. The arbitration proceedings are discontinued, pursuant to the second paragraph of Article 11(B); 7. The advance [on costs] relating to the costs of the arbitration is not paid, pursuant to the second paragraph of Article 16(C). Subject to the provisions of Article 14(B), the arbitral tribunal's mandate shall terminate upon the termination of the arbitral proceedings. Article 14 Form, content, correction, interpretation of the award, additional awards and receipt of written notifications An arbitral award shall contain the following: 1. the names, titles and addresses of the parties, their representatives [and attorneys]; 2. the legal grounds upon which the award is based, and the reasoning of the award, and the amount of any compensation awarded in compensation claims; 3. the place of arbitration and the date of the award; 4. the names, signature and any dissenting opinion of the members of the arbitral tribunal; 5. a statement that an action may be brought to set aside the award. Unless otherwise agreed, the arbitral tribunal may render partial awards. The arbitral award shall be notified to the parties by the sole arbitrator or the presiding arbitrator. The parties may request the arbitral award to be sent to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) upon payment of the relevant costs. In such cases, the sole arbitrator or the presiding arbitrator shall submit the award and the case file to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi), which shall be kept at the office of the court clerk. B) Within thirty days of receipt of the award, either party may, with notice to the counter party, request the arbitral tribunal: 1. to correct in the award any material errors in computation, any clerical[or typographical] errors or any errors of similar nature; 2. to interpret the entire or a part of the award. Where the arbitral tribunal considers, following receipt of the counter-party's views, the request to be justified, it shall correct the material error or provide the interpretation within thirty days of receipt of such request. The arbitral tribunal may correct any material error contained in the award on its own initiative within thirty days from the date of the award. Either party may, with notice to the counter-party and within thirty days of receipt of the award, request [the arbitral tribunal] to render an additional award to determine matters raised during the arbitral proceedings but omitted in the award rendered. Where the arbitral tribunal considers the request to be justified, it shall render the additional award within sixty days. Awards dealing with corrections and interpretations and any additional award shall be notified to the parties and form part of the arbitral award. C) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, any written communication shall be deemed to have been received when delivered to the addressee personally or when delivered at its domicile, habitual residence, place of business or mailing address. If none of the above places of notification can be ascertained following a reasonable inquiry, a written communication shall deemed received when sent to the addressee's last known domicile, habitual residence, place of business or mailing address by registered post or any other means which provides a record of the attempt to deliver. A written communication shall be deemed received on the date it is so delivered in accordance with the method foreseen. The provisions of this sub-article do not apply to notifications made by the court.

sections.title: Recourse Against Arbitral Awards
sections.text: Chapter Five. Recourse Against Arbitral Awards Article 15 Set aside claims and arbitral awards becoming enforceable A) Recourse against an arbitral award may be made only by an application to set aside the award. The set aside application shall be made to the regional judicial court which is competent with respect to the location of the competent civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) pursuant to Article 3, and shall be heard [by the court] expeditiously and as a matter of priority (Amended 2nd sentence: 28/02/2018 - 7101/53). An arbitral award may be set aside where: 1. The applicant furnishes proof that: a) a party to the arbitration agreement lacked the [required] legal capacity, or the arbitration agreement is invalid pursuant to the law agreed to by the parties or, failing such agreement, pursuant to Turkish law; b) the constitution of the arbitral tribunal was not made in accordance with the procedure determined in the parties' agreement or with the procedure set out in this Law; c) the arbitral award was not rendered within the arbitration period; d) the arbitral tribunal unlawfully found itself competent or incompetent; e) the arbitral tribunal has determined in its award on [a dispute or] a matter that falls beyond the scope of the arbitration agreement or failed to determine the entirety of the claim brought, or has exceeded its competence; f) the arbitral proceedings were not conducted in compliance with the parties' agreement as to the procedure or, absent such agreement, with the provisions contained in this Law, and that such non-compliance affected the substance of the award; g) the principle of equality of the parties was not adhered to; or 2. The ‘regional judicial court’ finds that (Amended phrase: 28/02/2018 - 7101/53): a) the subject-matter dispute addressed in the arbitral tribunal's award is not capable of settlement by arbitration (inarbitrable) under Turkish law; [or] b) the award violates public policy. Where an application to set aside is made on the ground that the award makes a determination in respect of matters that fall beyond the scope of the arbitration agreement, only the part(s) of the award dealing with the matters that fall beyond the scope of the arbitration agreement may be set aside, where such segregation is possible. The application to set aside an award may be made within thirty days. The period commences from the date the parties are notified of the award, or of the decision concerning its correction or interpretation, or of the additional award. A set aside application shall automatically suspend the enforcement of the arbitral award. The parties may, in part or in full, waive their right to initiate an action to set aside the award. Any party whose domicile or habitual residence is not in Turkey may completely waive the right to initiate an action to set aside the award by way of an express clause contained in the arbitration agreement or in a later written agreement [entered into after the execution of the arbitration agreement]; or they may waive the right to initiate an action to set aside the award only in respect of one or more of the grounds for set aside as set forth above [see Article 15A(2)(1.), above]. The application to set aside shall, unless the competent ‘regional judicial court’ (Amended phrase: 28/02/2018 - 7101/53) decides otherwise, be determined solely on the basis of the review of the documents. The judgment rendered in respect of the application to set aside the award may be appealed in accordance with the provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure, No: 6100 of 12 January 2011 (Amended 1st sentence: 28/02/2018 - 7101/53). The appeal review shall be limited to the grounds for set aside contained in this article, and shall be determined [by the court] expeditiously and as a matter of priority. Where the application to set aside the award is granted and [1] no appeal is launched against the judgment, or [2] in the event the judgment is granted on the basis of the existence of circumstances envisaged in paragraphs 1(b), (d), (e), (f) and (g) and/or paragraph 2(b) above, the parties may appoint [new] arbitrators and determine a new arbitration period, unless otherwise agreed by the parties. The parties may appoint the former arbitrators should they so desire. B) Once the court judgment dismissing the set aside application has become final, the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) shall, upon request, issue a certificate attesting that the arbitral award has become enforceable. The issuance of the certificate shall not be subject to the payment of any fees. In the event the arbitral award is enforced [via the execution offices], the provisions of the Code on Charges shall apply. Where the period foreseen for set aside applications has expired or the parties have waived the right to initiate an action to set aside the award, the provisions in sub-article A(2.)(a) and (b) shall be considered by the court ex officio when deciding to whether issue a certificate attesting that the arbitral award has become enforceable. In such cases, unless the court decides otherwise, the examination shall be made solely on the basis of the review of the documents.

sections.title: Costs of Arbitration
sections.text: Chapter 6. Costs of Arbitration Article 16 Arbitrator fees, costs of proceedings, payment of advance costs, and payment of costs A) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the fees of the arbitrators shall be fixed by agreement between the arbitral tribunal and the parties, having regard to the amount of receivable subject to dispute, the nature of the dispute and the [likely] period of the arbitral proceedings. The parties may determine the fees of the arbitrators by reference to established international rules or institutional arbitration rules. Where the parties and the arbitral tribunal cannot agree on the determination of the fees or the arbitration agreement does not contain any provision concerning the determination of the fees, or where no reference has been made by the parties in this respect to the established international rules or institutional arbitration rules, the fees of the arbitral tribunal shall be determined in accordance with the schedule of fees determined annually by the Ministry of Justice following consultations with the relevant professional organizations with public body status. Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the fees of the presiding arbitrator shall be calculated as ten percent more than the fee to be paid to each arbitrator. No additional fees shall be payable for the correction or interpretation of the award, or in connection with the issuance of an additional award. B) The arbitral tribunal shall state the costs of the arbitration in its arbitral award. The costs [of the arbitration or proceedings] shall include: 1. the fees of the arbitrators; 2. the arbitrators' travel and other expenses; 3. the fees paid to the arbitral tribunal appointed experts and other persons whose assistance was sought, as well as the costs of any site inspection; 4. the witnesses' travel and other expenses, to the extent approved by the arbitral tribunal; 5. the legal representation costs of the successful party, if any, as determined by the arbitral tribunal and calculated in accordance with the minimum fee schedule for attorneys; 6. court fees and charges payable in respect of applications to the courts made pursuant to this Law; 7. notification expenses with respect to the arbitral proceedings. C) The arbitral tribunal may request the claimant to deposit an advance for the arbitration costs. Where the advance is not paid within the period determined in the arbitral tribunal's decision, the arbitral tribunal may suspend the proceedings. The arbitral proceedings shall be continued should the advance be paid within thirty days from the notification to the parties of the suspension of the proceedings; otherwise, the arbitration shall terminate. Following the issuance of the arbitral award, the arbitral tribunal shall provide to the parties a document showing the places of expenditure and the amount of the advance paid, and return any remaining balance to the paying party. D) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the costs of the proceedings shall be borne by the unsuccessful party. Where both parties' claims are partially upheld in the arbitral award, the costs of the proceedings shall be apportioned among the parties having regard to their measure of rightfulness. The award of the arbitral tribunal terminating the arbitral proceedings or recording the settlement between the parties shall also set out the costs of the arbitral proceedings.

sections.title: Final Provisions
sections.text: Chapter 7. Final Provisions Article 17 Repealed and inapplicable provisions Unless otherwise stated, the provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure shall not apply with respect to the matters regulated by this Law. Article 5 of the Law Concerning the Principles to be adhered to in Respect of Arbitration Proceedings Arising from Concession Contracts Concerning Public Services, Law No. 4501 of 21 January 2000 is repealed. Additional Article 1 (Added 28/2/2018-7101/54). Competent court in respect of subject matter of dispute Duties and authorities granted to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) in this Law shall be exercised by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) or the commercial court of first instance (asliye ticaret mahkemesi), according to the subject matter of the dispute. Temporary Article 1 Where the parties and the arbitral tribunal cannot agree on the fees , or the arbitration agreement does not contain any provision regarding the determination of the fees or the parties have made no reference in respect of the fees to either internationally established rules or institutional arbitration rules, the fees of the arbitrators shall be determined by civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi), having regard to the nature of the dispute and the [likely] period of the arbitral proceedings, until such time the Ministry of Justice determines the relevant fee schedule. The principles and procedures regarding the preparation of the fee schedule shall be specified in the regulation to be issued by the Ministry of Justice within six months following the publication of this Law. Article 18 Entry in force This Law enters into force on the date of its publication. Article 19 Implementation The provisions of this Law shall be implemented by the Council of Ministers.

plain_text:
Previous version# For a copy of a previous version of this document, should this be available, please contact customer support.

INTERNATIONAL ARBITRATION LAW* Law No. 4686 of 21 June 2001. Official Gazette of 5 July 2001 No. 24453. In effect 5 July 2001. Unofficial translation by Prof. Ali Yesilirmak, Ms Utku Cosar and Dr Dogan Gultutan. The notes and explanations in square brackets have been added to the text by the translators solely for the purpose of providing the reader with further information and/or assistance.

Chapter One. General Provisions Article 1 Purpose and scope The purpose of this Law is to regulate procedural and substantive matters concerning international arbitration. This Law shall apply in cases where the dispute involves a foreign element and Turkey is determined as the place of arbitration, or where the provisions of this Law are agreed upon by the parties or the arbitral tribunal. Articles 5 and 6 of this Law shall apply even in cases where the place of arbitration determined is located outside Turkey. This Law shall not apply to disputes related to real rights (ayni haklar) concerning immovable property located in Turkey and disputes that are not subject to the parties' will. This Law shall also apply to the resolution, through international arbitration, of disputes arising from concession contracts relating to public services and which involve a foreign element, pursuant to the Law Concerning the Principles to be adhered to in Respect of Arbitration Proceedings Arising from Concession Contracts Concerning Public Services, Law No. 4501 of 21 January 2000. The provisions of international conventions to which Turkey is a party are reserved. Article 2 Foreign element The existence of any of the following circumstances shall indicate that the dispute involves a foreign element, in which case the arbitration shall be considered as international. 1. Where the parties to the arbitration agreement have their domiciles or habitual residences or places of business in different States. 2. Where the parties have their domicile, habitual residence or place of business in a State other than: a. the place of arbitration, specified in, or determined pursuant to, the arbitration agreement; [or] b. the place where a substantial part of the obligations arising from the underlying contract is to be performed or the place most closely connected to the subject matter of the dispute. 3. Where at least one of the shareholders of the company that is a party to the underlying contract constituting the basis for the arbitration agreement has brought foreign capital [into Turkey] in accordance with the laws concerning the encouragement of foreign capital or where the execution of a loan and/or guarantee agreement(s) for the purposes of procuring capital from abroad is required for the implementation of the said underlying contract. 4. Where the underlying contract constituting the basis for the arbitration agreement or the legal relationship results in the cross-border movement of capital or goods. The provisions of Law No. 4501 of 21 January 2000 are reserved. Article 3 Competent Court and Extent of Court Intervention In respect of court intervention or assistance foreseen in this Law, the competent court shall be the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) located at the respondent's domicile, habitual residence or place of business; where the respondent does not have its domicile, habitual residence or place of business in Turkey, the competent court shall be the Istanbul Civil Court of First Instance (Istanbul Asliye Hukuk Mahkemesi). In respect of matters concerning international arbitration, the courts may only intervene in accordance with the provisions of this Law.

Chapter Two. Arbitration Agreement Article 4 Definition and Form An arbitration agreement is an agreement by the parties to submit to arbitration all or certain disputes, whether contractual or otherwise, which have arisen or which may arise between them in respect of a current legal relationship. An arbitration agreement may be in the form of an arbitration clause in the underlying contract or in the form of a separate agreement. The arbitration agreement shall be in writing. In order for the written form requirement to be considered satisfied, the arbitration agreement must be recorded in a written document signed by the parties or in a means of communication such as letters, telegram, telex, facsimile exchanged by the parties or in electronic environment, or the existence of a written arbitration agreement is alleged in the statement of claim and this is not objected by the respondent in its statement of defence. A reference [in the underlying contract] to a document containing an arbitration clause shall constitute an arbitration agreement provided that such reference was for the purpose of incorporating the clause into the underlying contract. The arbitration agreement shall be valid if it is in accordance with the law agreed by the parties as applicable to the arbitration agreement, failing such agreement, with Turkish law. No objection shall be made against the arbitration agreement on the basis of the invalidity of the underlying contract or that the arbitration agreement relates to a dispute not yet arisen. Article 5 Arbitration objection and agreement to arbitrate before the court If an action is brought before the court concerning a matter which is the subject of an arbitration agreement, the counter-party may raise an arbitration objection. The provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure relating to preliminary objections shall apply to the assertion of arbitration objections and the resolution of disputes concerning the validity of the arbitration agreement. In case the court upholds an arbitration objection, it shall dismiss the action on procedural grounds. In case the parties agree to arbitrate during court proceedings, the case file shall be sent by the court to the relevant arbitral tribunal. Article 6 Interim measures of protection and interim attachments It is not incompatible with an arbitration agreement for a party to request, before or during arbitral proceedings, from a court an interim measure of protection or an interim attachment, and for a court to grant such measure or attachment. Unless otherwise agreed, the arbitral tribunal may, at the request of a party, order interim measures of protection or interim attachments during arbitral proceedings. The arbitral tribunal may subject granting interim measures of protection or interim attachment to the provision of an appropriate security. The arbitral tribunal shall not be empowered to grant interim measures of protection or attachments [a] that are required to be enforced through execution offices or to be executed via other official authorities or [b] that bind third parties. Where a party fails to comply with the interim measure of protection or attachment order made by the arbitral tribunal, the counter-party may request the competent court's assistance in connection with the issuance of an order for interim measure of protection or an interim attachment. The competent court may, where required, hear [the case in question] through a substitute court. The parties' right to make requests in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure and the Code of Execution is reserved. Any interim measures of protection or interim attachments granted by the court prior to or during arbitral proceedings on the application of a party shall automatically cease to have effect once the award of the arbitral tribunal becomes enforceable or the arbitral tribunal dismisses the claim.

Chapter Three. Arbitral Tribunal's Appointment, Challenge, Responsibility, Termination of its Duties and its Competence Article 7 Number of arbitrators, their appointment, challenge, responsibility, termination of duties and competence A) The parties are entitled to determine the number of arbitrators. However, the number shall be an odd number. Where the parties have not determined the number of arbitrators, three arbitrators shall be appointed. B) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the following principles apply to the appointment of arbitrators: 1. Only natural persons can be appointed as arbitrators. 2. Where a sole arbitrator is to be appointed and the parties are unable to agree on the arbitrator, the arbitrator shall be appointed, upon request of a party, by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi). 3. Where three arbitrators are to be appointed, each party shall appoint one arbitrator and the two co-arbitrators thus appointed shall appoint the third arbitrator. Where a party fails to appoint an arbitrator within thirty days of receipt of a request to do so from the other party, or where the two co-arbitrators fail to agree on the third arbitrator within thirty days of their appointment, the appointment shall be made, upon request of a party, by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi). The third arbitrator appointed shall be the chairman of the arbitral tribunal. 4. Where more than three arbitrators are to be appointed, the arbitrators who are to appoint the final arbitrator shall be appointed by the parties in equal numbers in accordance with the procedure set forth in the above paragraph. Where, despite an agreement on the appointment procedure relating to arbitrators; 1. a party fails to comply with that agreement; 2. the parties or the arbitrators appointed by them are unable to agree under such procedure in respect of arbitrator appointment, despite being required to decide jointly on the arbitrator appointment under the agreed procedure; [or] 3. a third party, institution or body empowered to appoint arbitrators fails to appoint the arbitrator(s), the appointment of the arbitral tribunal shall be made, upon a party request, by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi). The civil court of first instance's decision rendered pursuant to these sub-articles, upon hearing the parties where necessary, shall be final. The court of first instance, in appointing an arbitrator, shall give due regard to: [1] the parties' agreement, [2] the principles regarding [the requirement for] arbitrator's independence and impartiality, and the need for the sole arbitrator or, where there are to be three arbitrators, for two arbitrators not to be of the same nationality as a party in cases where the parties are of different nationalities. The above procedure shall similarly apply where more than three arbitrators are to be appointed. C) Any person offered appointment as an arbitrator shall, prior to accepting appointment, disclose any circumstances likely to give rise to justifiable doubts as to its impartiality or independence. Provided that the parties have not already been informed, the arbitrator shall inform the parties of any such circumstances thereafter arising without delay. An arbitrator may be challenged in cases where: [1] it does not possess the qualifications required pursuant to the parties' agreement, [2] a ground for challenge foreseen in the arbitration procedure agreed upon by the parties exists, or [3] circumstances which give rise to justifiable doubts as to its impartiality or independence have arisen. D) The parties are entitled to agree on the procedure in respect of challenges against arbitrators. A party who intends to challenge an arbitrator may, within thirty days following the constitution of the arbitral tribunal or its awareness of the circumstance that may entitle a challenge against the arbitrator, submit its challenge, which challenge must be notified to the counter party by writing. A party who challenges one or more arbitrators shall notify the arbitral tribunal of its request and reasons for the challenge. Where the challenge is dismissed, the requesting party may apply to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi), within thirty days of becoming aware of the dismissal decision, to request an order that the arbitral tribunal's dismissal decision be revoked and the arbitrator(s) be removed. Any request to remove the entire arbitral tribunal or the decision-making majority may only be made to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi). The civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi)'s decisions rendered pursuant to this sub-article are final. Where the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) orders the removal of the entire arbitral tribunal or the decision-making majority, the arbitration proceedings shall terminate. Unless the arbitrator(s) has been designated by name in the arbitration agreement, the arbitrator appointment process shall be repeated. E) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, an arbitrator who accepts appointment shall be obliged to indemnify the parties for any damages suffered due to its avoidance of its duties without a justified reason. F) If an arbitrator becomes de jure or de facto unable to perform its functions, timely or at all, its mandate shall terminate upon its withdrawal or the parties agreement to that effect. Either party may request the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) to decide on the termination of the arbitrator's mandate should the parties disagree as to whether circumstances exist to require the arbitrator to withdraw from office. The decision rendered by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) shall be final. The arbitrator's withdrawal from office or the counter party's consent to the termination of the arbitrator's mandate shall not constitute the acceptance as to the existence of grounds for challenge. G) Where the mandate of an arbitrator terminates for any reason whatsoever, a replacement arbitrator shall be appointed in accordance with the rules that were applicable to the appointment of the arbitrator being replaced. The replacement of one or more arbitrators shall not suspend the arbitration period. However, where the arbitration agreement designates the arbitrator(s) by name, the arbitration proceedings shall terminate where the mandate of the arbitral tribunal or the decision-making majority terminates for any reason whatsoever. H) The arbitral tribunal may rule on its own jurisdiction, including any objections with respect to the existence or validity of the arbitration agreement. When determining such, the arbitration clause, where forming part of an underlying contract, shall be treated independent of the other terms of the underlying contract. A decision by the arbitral tribunal declaring the underlying contract null and void shall not entail ipso jure the invalidity of the arbitration agreement [contained within it]. Any challenge against the arbitral tribunal's jurisdiction shall be raised with the first statement of defence at the latest. The parties are not precluded from raising such a challenge due to their appointment of the arbitrator(s) or participation in the appointment process. Any challenge against the arbitral tribunal on the basis that it has exceeded the scope of its authority shall not be valid unless raised immediately. The arbitral tribunal may, in respect of either case described above, admit a delayed challenge where it considers the delay justified. The arbitral tribunal shall determine an objection relating to its lack of jurisdiction as a preliminary issue. Where the arbitral tribunal determines that it has jurisdiction, it shall proceed with the arbitration and render its award.

Chapter Four. Arbitral Procedure Article 8 Determination of the rules of procedure, equal treatment of parties and their representation A) Subject to the mandatory provisions of this Law, the parties are entitled to determine the rules of arbitral procedure to be applied by the arbitral tribunal, or determine such by way of reference to a law or international or institutional arbitration rules. Absent such party agreement, the arbitral tribunal shall conduct the arbitral proceedings in accordance with the provisions of this Law. B) The parties possess equal rights and authority in respect of the arbitral proceedings. The parties shall be afforded the [full] opportunity to assert their claims and defences. The parties may also be represented in arbitration proceedings by foreign natural or legal persons. This provision shall not apply in respect of requests made to a court concerning arbitration. Article 9 Place of arbitration The place of arbitration shall be freely determined by the parties or the arbitral institution agreed upon. Absent such agreement, the place of arbitration shall be determined by the arbitral tribunal having regard to the circumstances of the case. The arbitral tribunal may meet at a place other than the place of arbitration where the circumstances of the arbitral proceedings so require, provided that prior notice is given to the parties. Article 10 Commencement, period and language of arbitration, statements of claim and defence, and the terms of reference A) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, an arbitration shall be deemed commenced [1] on the date a request is made for the appointment of the arbitrator(s) to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) or to the person, institution or body the parties have agreed upon to appoint the arbitrator(s); [2] where both parties are responsible for the appointment of the arbitrators pursuant to the [arbitration] agreement, on the date the claimant notifies the counter-party of its arbitrator appointment and invites the counter-party to appoint its own arbitrator; or [3] where the arbitration agreement designates the arbitrators by name, on the date the counter-party receives the request for arbitration. Where a party has obtained an interim measure of protection or an interim attachment from a court, it shall commence arbitration proceedings within thirty days [as from the date the measure or attachment is granted]. Otherwise, the interim measure or the interim attachment shall automatically cease to have any effect. B) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the arbitral tribunal shall render its award on the merits within one year starting from the date of appointment of the arbitrator in cases with sole arbitrator, within one year starting from the date of the issuance of the first meeting minutes by the arbitral tribunal in cases with more than one arbitrator. The period of arbitration may be extended by party agreement, failing which by the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) upon application by either party. Where the request for extension is dismissed, the arbitral proceedings shall terminate on the date the period of arbitration expires. The court's decision [on extension] shall be final. C) The language of the arbitration shall be Turkish or any other language that is the official language of a state recognized by the Republic of Turkey. Absent party agreement as regards the language or languages of the [arbitral] proceedings, such shall be determined by the arbitral tribunal. Unless otherwise agreed by the parties or determined by the arbitral tribunal in its relevant interim decision on the matter, such language(s) shall be utilised in respect of the parties' all written statements, the hearing(s), the arbitral tribunal's interim or final awards, and also in its [all other] written communications. The arbitral tribunal may order that any documents relied upon by the parties shall be accompanied by a translation into the language(s) used in the arbitration proceedings. D) Within the time period agreed by the parties or as determined by the arbitral tribunal, [1] the claimant shall submit to the arbitral tribunal its statement of claim containing [the particulars as to] the parties' names, titles and addresses, its legal representative, the arbitration clause or agreement, the contract or legal relationship giving rise or relating to the dispute, the circumstances upon which the claim is based, the subject matter of the dispute, the amount in dispute and its prayer for relief; [2] the respondent shall similarly submit to the arbitral tribunal its statement of defence. The parties may submit together with their statements written evidence on which they rely and may refer to the evidence they intend to submit at a later stage. Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, either party may amend or expand its claim or defence during the course of the arbitral proceedings, unless the arbitral tribunal considers it inappropriate to allow such amending or expansion having regard to its belated nature or on the ground that such will cause an unjustifiable and significant difficulty to the counter-party, and having regard to the other relevant circumstances. The claim or defence, cannot be amended or expanded where such would exceed the scope of the arbitration agreement. E) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the arbitral tribunal shall, following the filing of the statements of claim and defence, draw up its terms of reference. The terms of reference shall contain such particulars as the parties' names and titles, their addresses for notification during arbitral proceedings, a summary of their claims and defences, their prayers for relief, explanations as regards the dispute, the names, titles, and addresses of the arbitrators, the place of arbitration, the period of arbitration and the date of its commencement, explanations as regards the procedural provisions applicable to the dispute, and whether the arbitrators have authority to act as amiable compositeur. The terms of reference shall be signed by the arbitral tribunal and the parties. Article 11 Hearing and on-paper arbitral proceedings, loss of party status, non-participation of a party in arbitral proceedings A) The arbitral tribunal shall decide whether to hold hearings for the reasons such as the presentation of evidence, the submission of oral arguments or requests for explanations from experts, or whether to conduct the arbitral proceedings solely on the basis of review of documents. Unless the parties have agreed that no hearings shall be held, the arbitral tribunal shall hold a hearing(s) at an appropriate stage of the proceedings where requested by a party. The arbitral tribunal shall provide notice to the parties sufficiently in advance concerning the date of any site-inspection relating to the dispute, expert examination or any hearing and meeting of the arbitral tribunal to be held for the purposes of examining other evidence, and as to the consequences of the failure to attend such. All statements, information or other documents submitted to the arbitral tribunal by one party shall be communicated to the parties. B) Where a party to the arbitration loses its party status, the arbitral tribunal shall postpone the arbitral proceedings and notify the concerned for the purposes of the continuation of the proceedings. In this case, the arbitration period shall stop to run during the postponement. The arbitral proceedings shall terminate where, within six months [following the postponement], [1] no notification is made [within the meaning of the above sub-article] or [2] despite such notification being made, the notifying party fails to notify in express terms the counter-party or the arbitral tribunal of its intention to continue with the arbitral proceedings. C) Where a party does not participate in the arbitration proceedings, the following provisions shall apply: 1. Where the claimant fails to submit its statement of claim within the period provided without a valid reason, the arbitral tribunal shall terminate the arbitral proceedings. 2. Where the statement of claim is not in accordance with the first paragraph of Article 10(D) and the deficiency is not remedied within the period determined by the arbitral tribunal, the tribunal shall terminate the arbitral proceedings. 3. Where the respondent fails to submit its statement of defence, the arbitral proceedings shall continue, and the failure shall not be considered as constituting the respondent's acceptance of the claimant's claims. 4. Where either party fails to attend a hearing or produce its evidence without a valid reason, the arbitral tribunal may continue the arbitral proceedings and render its award on the basis of the evidence submitted. Article 12 Expert appointment by the arbitral tribunal, taking of evidence, laws applicable to the merits of the dispute and settlement A) The arbitral tribunal may: 1. appoint one or more experts to produce a report on specific issues determined by the arbitral tribunal; 2. require the parties to make necessary explanations to the expert(s), [produce or] provide access to the expert(s) to any relevant information or documents; 3. order an on-site inspection to be conducted. Unless otherwise agreed, where a party so requests or the arbitral tribunal considers it necessary, the expert(s) shall, after delivery of its written or oral reports [on the relevant matters], attend the hearing to which they are invited. At the hearing, the parties shall have the opportunity to put questions to the expert(s) and to have their party-appointed experts heard on matters in dispute. B) The parties shall submit their evidence within the time period determined by the arbitral tribunal. The arbitral tribunal may request assistance from the court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) in respect of the taking evidence. In such cases the court shall apply the provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure. C) The arbitral tribunal shall render its award in accordance with the terms of the contract between the parties and the law chosen by them to govern the merits of the dispute. In the interpretation of contractual terms and when filling any lacuna in respect of such, the commercial usage and customs and business practices relating to the chosen law shall be considered. Unless otherwise stated, a choice as to the laws of a state shall be construed as a choice as regards the substantive laws of that state, and not its conflict of laws rules or its rules of procedure. In the absence of party agreement as regards the law applicable to the merits of the dispute, the arbitral tribunal shall render its award according to the substantive laws of the state it considers most closely connected to the dispute. The arbitral tribunal shall decide ex aequo et bono or as amiable compositeur only where expressly so authorised by the parties. D) Where the parties settle the dispute during arbitral proceedings, the arbitral proceedings shall be terminated. The arbitral tribunal shall, upon request and where such request is deemed appropriate, record the settlement in the form of an arbitral award [on terms agreed]. Article 13 Arbitral tribunal's decision-making procedure, and termination of arbitral proceedings A) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the arbitral tribunal shall decide by a majority of all its members. Where authorised by the parties or the other members of the arbitral tribunal, the presiding arbitrator may determine certain issues concerning the arbitral procedure on its own. B) The arbitral proceedings shall terminate upon the issuance of a final award or on the occurrence of any of the following circumstances: 1. The claimant's withdrawal of its claim, unless the respondent objects and the arbitral tribunal accepts that the latter possesses a legal interest in the final resolution of the dispute; 2. The parties agree to terminate the arbitral proceedings; 3. The arbitral tribunal considers the continuation of the arbitral proceedings unnecessary or impossible due to any other reasons; 4. The court dismisses a request to extend the arbitration period, pursuant to the second paragraph of Article 10(B); 5. The arbitral tribunal fails to render its award with unanimity, despite the parties' agreement to that effect; 6. The arbitration proceedings are discontinued, pursuant to the second paragraph of Article 11(B); 7. The advance [on costs] relating to the costs of the arbitration is not paid, pursuant to the second paragraph of Article 16(C). Subject to the provisions of Article 14(B), the arbitral tribunal's mandate shall terminate upon the termination of the arbitral proceedings. Article 14 Form, content, correction, interpretation of the award, additional awards and receipt of written notifications An arbitral award shall contain the following: 1. the names, titles and addresses of the parties, their representatives [and attorneys]; 2. the legal grounds upon which the award is based, and the reasoning of the award, and the amount of any compensation awarded in compensation claims; 3. the place of arbitration and the date of the award; 4. the names, signature and any dissenting opinion of the members of the arbitral tribunal; 5. a statement that an action may be brought to set aside the award. Unless otherwise agreed, the arbitral tribunal may render partial awards. The arbitral award shall be notified to the parties by the sole arbitrator or the presiding arbitrator. The parties may request the arbitral award to be sent to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) upon payment of the relevant costs. In such cases, the sole arbitrator or the presiding arbitrator shall submit the award and the case file to the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi), which shall be kept at the office of the court clerk. B) Within thirty days of receipt of the award, either party may, with notice to the counter party, request the arbitral tribunal: 1. to correct in the award any material errors in computation, any clerical[or typographical] errors or any errors of similar nature; 2. to interpret the entire or a part of the award. Where the arbitral tribunal considers, following receipt of the counter-party's views, the request to be justified, it shall correct the material error or provide the interpretation within thirty days of receipt of such request. The arbitral tribunal may correct any material error contained in the award on its own initiative within thirty days from the date of the award. Either party may, with notice to the counter-party and within thirty days of receipt of the award, request [the arbitral tribunal] to render an additional award to determine matters raised during the arbitral proceedings but omitted in the award rendered. Where the arbitral tribunal considers the request to be justified, it shall render the additional award within sixty days. Awards dealing with corrections and interpretations and any additional award shall be notified to the parties and form part of the arbitral award. C) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, any written communication shall be deemed to have been received when delivered to the addressee personally or when delivered at its domicile, habitual residence, place of business or mailing address. If none of the above places of notification can be ascertained following a reasonable inquiry, a written communication shall deemed received when sent to the addressee's last known domicile, habitual residence, place of business or mailing address by registered post or any other means which provides a record of the attempt to deliver. A written communication shall be deemed received on the date it is so delivered in accordance with the method foreseen. The provisions of this sub-article do not apply to notifications made by the court.

Chapter Five. Recourse Against Arbitral Awards Article 15 Set aside claims and arbitral awards becoming enforceable A) Recourse against an arbitral award may be made only by an application to set aside the award. The set aside application shall be made to the regional judicial court which is competent with respect to the location of the competent civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) pursuant to Article 3, and shall be heard [by the court] expeditiously and as a matter of priority (Amended 2nd sentence: 28/02/2018 - 7101/53). An arbitral award may be set aside where: 1. The applicant furnishes proof that: a) a party to the arbitration agreement lacked the [required] legal capacity, or the arbitration agreement is invalid pursuant to the law agreed to by the parties or, failing such agreement, pursuant to Turkish law; b) the constitution of the arbitral tribunal was not made in accordance with the procedure determined in the parties' agreement or with the procedure set out in this Law; c) the arbitral award was not rendered within the arbitration period; d) the arbitral tribunal unlawfully found itself competent or incompetent; e) the arbitral tribunal has determined in its award on [a dispute or] a matter that falls beyond the scope of the arbitration agreement or failed to determine the entirety of the claim brought, or has exceeded its competence; f) the arbitral proceedings were not conducted in compliance with the parties' agreement as to the procedure or, absent such agreement, with the provisions contained in this Law, and that such non-compliance affected the substance of the award; g) the principle of equality of the parties was not adhered to; or 2. The ‘regional judicial court’ finds that (Amended phrase: 28/02/2018 - 7101/53): a) the subject-matter dispute addressed in the arbitral tribunal's award is not capable of settlement by arbitration (inarbitrable) under Turkish law; [or] b) the award violates public policy. Where an application to set aside is made on the ground that the award makes a determination in respect of matters that fall beyond the scope of the arbitration agreement, only the part(s) of the award dealing with the matters that fall beyond the scope of the arbitration agreement may be set aside, where such segregation is possible. The application to set aside an award may be made within thirty days. The period commences from the date the parties are notified of the award, or of the decision concerning its correction or interpretation, or of the additional award. A set aside application shall automatically suspend the enforcement of the arbitral award. The parties may, in part or in full, waive their right to initiate an action to set aside the award. Any party whose domicile or habitual residence is not in Turkey may completely waive the right to initiate an action to set aside the award by way of an express clause contained in the arbitration agreement or in a later written agreement [entered into after the execution of the arbitration agreement]; or they may waive the right to initiate an action to set aside the award only in respect of one or more of the grounds for set aside as set forth above [see Article 15A(2)(1.), above]. The application to set aside shall, unless the competent ‘regional judicial court’ (Amended phrase: 28/02/2018 - 7101/53) decides otherwise, be determined solely on the basis of the review of the documents. The judgment rendered in respect of the application to set aside the award may be appealed in accordance with the provisions of the Code of Civil Procedure, No: 6100 of 12 January 2011 (Amended 1st sentence: 28/02/2018 - 7101/53). The appeal review shall be limited to the grounds for set aside contained in this article, and shall be determined [by the court] expeditiously and as a matter of priority. Where the application to set aside the award is granted and [1] no appeal is launched against the judgment, or [2] in the event the judgment is granted on the basis of the existence of circumstances envisaged in paragraphs 1(b), (d), (e), (f) and (g) and/or paragraph 2(b) above, the parties may appoint [new] arbitrators and determine a new arbitration period, unless otherwise agreed by the parties. The parties may appoint the former arbitrators should they so desire. B) Once the court judgment dismissing the set aside application has become final, the civil court of first instance (asliye hukuk mahkemesi) shall, upon request, issue a certificate attesting that the arbitral award has become enforceable. The issuance of the certificate shall not be subject to the payment of any fees. In the event the arbitral award is enforced [via the execution offices], the provisions of the Code on Charges shall apply. Where the period foreseen for set aside applications has expired or the parties have waived the right to initiate an action to set aside the award, the provisions in sub-article A(2.)(a) and (b) shall be considered by the court ex officio when deciding to whether issue a certificate attesting that the arbitral award has become enforceable. In such cases, unless the court decides otherwise, the examination shall be made solely on the basis of the review of the documents.

Chapter 6. Costs of Arbitration Article 16 Arbitrator fees, costs of proceedings, payment of advance costs, and payment of costs A) Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, the fees of the arbitrators shall be fixed by agreement between the arbitral tribunal and the parties, having regard to the amount of receivable subject to dispute, the nature of the dispute and the [likely] period of the arbitral proceedings. The parties may determine the fees of the arbitrators by reference to established international rules or institutional arbitration rules.
